reviews.review_msg: Get ready for this course to wreck your gpa and pride. The TAs feel entirely useless but peter himself seems like an awesome guy. He allowed me to redo a paper my ta marked me POORLY on, that said ta set me up for failure for. This redo changed the letter grade overnight and I am in deep gratitude for dr Cramer. I have lots of stories.

For an English course there wasn't a whole lot of reading, which I was grateful for. Cramer is also just a really nice guy and super approachable. My TA for this class was also excellent. I found the assignments were a little complicated to understand but I'd take them any day over a final. There was no final, no midterm which was awesome.
Lectures are long, assignments can be so hard understand from the instructions.

a little dry, but he does his best. very knowledgeable and accessible outside of class. he does talk a lot tho. he didn't let me have an extension after my dad died bc of the course policy, which rlly messed with my head, but i guess i can understand.
My favourite prof that I've had so far. Class is fun as long you participate. If you keep up with the readings and attend class there is no reason you can't get a decent grade in his classes

Dry, but not a bad professor. The lectures are 3 hours long, and most of class is based on discussion on concepts that are difficult to understand sometimes. His rubrics are extremely vague and difficult to adhere to.

Dr. Cramer is a wonderful professor who listens to his student's concerns and welcomes questions about course material. Take a rhetoric class with him if you can! It is super useful for future English classes and 3 hour seminars are fun as long as you make an effort to participate.

Peter's the most humble and down-to-earth prof I've ever met. The class content and readings are difficult, but he takes the time to walk us through and listen to everyone's input during discussions. His classes are an absolute delight. Take the time to go to his office hours, too.

214 had very dry readings and lectures could be quite boring and lengthy. However, I feel this course was very helpful and I learned a lot. Would definitely take again. Peter is a fair marker and kind to his students. Doing the readings before class and participating is a must.
